heroCases.title: Andersen 400 Casement — Seal Failure / Water Intrusion

timeline.note: Called in — Homeowner reporting water intrusion around casement frame. ABC internal case WO-2026-001 created. Mike notified of intake #, sales rep Larry Sullivan CC'd.
Intake confirmation email sent to Mike Bergstrom and Larry Sullivan. Case # WO-2026-001 included.

Tech assessment — drove 22 min to site. Inspection checklist completed. Seal failure confirmed as manufacturer defect. Pre-visit photos captured. Measurement taken for custom seal replacement. Drive time: 44 min. Onsite: 1.5 hrs.
Warranty seal kit ordered from Andersen manufacturer. PO submitted in Agility. ETA: 12-14 business days. SMS sent to Sarah Kowalski: 'Parts ordered. ETA 2 weeks. We'll reach out when parts arrive.'
Automated 1-week check-in SMS to Sarah Kowalski: 'Hi Sarah, this is ABC Supply. Your window parts are on order and on track. We'll contact you to schedule installation as soon as they arrive.'
Seal kit received at warehouse. Parts verified. Case status updated to 'Ready to Schedule'. SMS to Sarah Kowalski: 'Great news — your parts arrived! We will call tomorrow to schedule your repair.'
Called Sarah Kowalski. Scheduled for Tuesday March 31 at 9am. Appointment confirmation SMS sent with tech name (Derek Olson) and estimated arrival.
'Heads up! Derek from ABC Supply is on his way. ETA ~25 min based on current traffic. Reply STOP to cancel or call 952-894-0100 to reschedule.'

Arrived on site. Opened work order on Field Service mobile. Logged arrival time. Completed installation checklist. Replaced seal. Window tested — no further water intrusion. Post-install photos captured. Completion report generated.
Work order closed. Completion report emailed to Mike Bergstrom and Sarah Kowalski with pre/post photos, time log, parts used, and warranty documentation. Case resolved.

heroCases.title: Pella Impervia Slider — Operator Mechanism Failure

timeline.note: Email received to service@abcsupply.com. Pella slider — operator stripped, can't lock. D365 auto-created case WO-2026-002. Routed to coordinator queue.
Confirmation email to Tyler with case # and expected response time (same business day).
Operator mechanism PART-OPR-MEC confirmed in stock. No parts order needed. Scheduled Derek for Thursday April 10 at 10am.
SMS to homeowner James Paulsen: 'ABC Supply scheduled for tomorrow (Thu Apr 10) between 9-11am. Tech: Derek Olson. Reply C to confirm or call 952-894-0100 to reschedule.'
Replied 'C' — appointment confirmed. Auto-reply sent: 'Confirmed! We'll send a heads-up when Derek is on his way.'
Derek en route SMS to James: 'Derek is 18 min away. ETA 10:06am.'
Arrived. Opened work order mobile. Captured pre-work photo. Replaced operator mechanism. Tested lock and slide operation. Post-work photo captured. Time: drive 31 min, onsite 1.1 hrs.
Case resolved. Completion report with photos emailed to Tyler Schroeder and James Paulsen. Job costing: 1 part ($89) + 1.5 hrs labor + 1 hr drive.
